evals.name: strategic-vendor-six-dimension
evals.prompt: Assess our vendor KIFTL (Khan Industrial Fasteners & Tools Ltd). They supply all our custom-machined steel fasteners — we have no alternative supplier and qualification of a new one would take 9 months. Annual spend is GBP 1.2M. They've been our supplier for 12 years. Their OTD is 94% (our threshold is 95%), quality rejection is 1.1%, they operate from a single site in Lahore, Pakistan. Their ISO 9001 expires in 60 days. Last filed accounts show revenue decline of 8% YoY. We have no visibility of their sub-suppliers.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive six-dimension vendor assessment classifying KIFTL as Strategic (Tier 1) due to high spend and no alternatives, with RED and AMBER flags across multiple dimensions, and a ranked action list addressing the most critical risks first.
expectations: Produces the mandatory output header with TASK, VENDOR TIER (Strategic), CONFIGURATION, and DATA SOURCES fields — every vendor assessment output must begin with this header

Classifies KIFTL as STRATEGIC (Tier 1) based on high spend (GBP 1.2M) and no alternatives — the Kraljic matrix places high-spend/no-alternative vendors in the Strategic quadrant with quarterly review frequency

Flags Operational dimension as AMBER for OTD at 94% vs 95% threshold — the skill marks OTD below configured threshold for Strategic vendors as RED, but 94% vs 95% is borderline and should at minimum be AMBER with declining trend concern
Flags the single production site in Lahore as AMBER for business continuity risk — single site operation with no documented BCP is an AMBER flag in the Operational dimension
Flags Compliance dimension as AMBER for ISO 9001 expiring in 60 days — the skill defines certification expiring within 90 days as AMBER requiring renewal evidence
Marks Tier 2 visibility (Strategic dimension) as a gap requiring immediate action — the skill states NEVER skip the Tier 2 visibility check for Strategic vendors; no mapping means UNASSESSED risk
Includes a ranked recommended actions list with owners and deadlines — the skill requires actions ranked by urgency, not just risk ratings

evals.name: bottleneck-vendor-classification
evals.prompt: Classify a vendor with annual spend of only GBP 15,000 who is our sole source for a custom-machined component with a 9-month qualification lead time for alternatives. We've never reviewed them before. What tier should they be and what assessment do they need?
evals.expected_output: Classification as BOTTLENECK (Tier 4) — the most dangerous and most neglected category. Despite low spend, the sole-source dependency with no qualified alternative and long qualification time makes this vendor high-risk.
expectations: Classifies the vendor as BOTTLENECK (Tier 4) — the skill's classification rule explicitly states: when assessing a vendor with low spend but sole-source dependency, ALWAYS classify as Bottleneck and flag as high-risk regardless of spend volume

Does NOT classify as Commodity based on low spend — the skill explicitly warns NEVER classify a sole-source supplier as low risk based on spend alone; operational dependency must be assessed separately
Identifies the 9-month qualification time as a critical risk factor — if this vendor fails, the business has no alternative for 9 months, making the impact of vendor failure disproportionate to spend
Recommends quarterly review frequency despite low spend — Bottleneck vendors get the same review frequency as Strategic vendors because their impact is equally critical
Recommends immediate actions: qualify an alternative supplier, request financial data, and map Tier 2 sub-suppliers — the vendor has never been reviewed, so all assessment dimensions start from zero

evals.name: negative-invoice-reconciliation-redirect
evals.prompt: I received an invoice from TechParts for GBP 45,000 but the price per unit is 5% above the PO. The goods receipt confirms the right quantities. Should I pay the full amount or dispute the price variance?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this is an invoice reconciliation question (three-way match with price variance), not a vendor assessment, and redirects to the invoice-reconciliation skill.
expectations: Recognizes that invoice price variance reconciliation is invoice-reconciliation skill territory — the skill description explicitly states 'NOT for: invoice reconciliation (use invoice-reconciliation)'
Redirects to the invoice-reconciliation skill or /invoice-reconcile command — invoice reconciliation has the three-way match workflow and tolerance-based exception routing
Does NOT attempt to process the invoice or determine whether to pay — vendor assessment evaluates vendor quality and risk; it does not process individual transactions

evals.name: negative-spend-analysis-redirect
evals.prompt: Can you analyze our vendor spend across all categories? I want to see which categories have too many vendors and where we can consolidate to save money.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this is a spend analysis question (category spend and vendor consolidation), not a vendor assessment, and redirects to the spend-analysis skill.
expectations: Recognizes that cross-category spend analysis and vendor consolidation are spend-analysis skill territory — the skill description explicitly states 'NOT for: spend category analysis (use spend-analysis)'
Redirects to the spend-analysis skill or /spend-analysis command — spend analysis has the category overview and consolidation analysis frameworks
Does NOT attempt to produce spend-by-category analysis — vendor assessment evaluates individual vendors across six dimensions; it does not aggregate spend data
